embed.title: !untrack
embed.description: Remove pokemon tracking entries. Also available as `{{prefix}}track remove <pokemon>` if you prefer that style.

fields.name: Usage
fields.value:
`{{prefix}}untrack pikachu` — remove all pikachu tracking
`{{prefix}}untrack pikachu charmander` — remove multiple
`{{prefix}}untrack everything` — wipe every pokemon tracking entry in your current profile
`{{prefix}}untrack id:45` — remove one specific entry by its UID (see `{{prefix}}tracked` — each row shows `[id:XX]`)

fields.name: Targeted removal by ID
fields.value: `{{prefix}}untrack id:45` is the safest way to remove a single rule without affecting other entries for the same pokemon. Use `{{prefix}}tracked` to see every rule's ID before using it. `{{prefix}}untrack id:45` also works for raid, egg, quest, etc.; it's not pokemon-specific.

fields.name: Removing by filter
fields.value:
`{{prefix}}untrack gen:1` — remove all Kanto pokemon tracking
`{{prefix}}untrack type:dragon` — remove all dragon-type tracking

Note: this removes any rule that named a matching pokemon, not rules containing the filter itself.

fields.name: Other commands have their own remove form
fields.value:
`{{prefix}}raid remove <pokemon|level>`
`{{prefix}}egg remove <level>`
`{{prefix}}quest remove <reward>`
`{{prefix}}lure remove <type>`
…and so on for every tracking type. Or use `{{prefix}}untrack id:<N>` which works regardless of type.
